Term Sheet — Proposed issuance of secured convertible debentures by Lanthorn Bio Public Company Limited (Issuer), to Tamarind Growth Fund V (Cayman) LP (Investor).
Securities context: Issuer is listed on Bangkok Meridian Exchange (BMX); provisional code BMX:NLB-DB26A; mock ISIN XX-NOT-VALID (placeholders only).
Instrument: Thai baht–denominated secured convertible debentures, tenor up to 3 years, conversion into new ordinary shares subject to TSEA approval; no pricing disclosed herein.
Use of proceeds: working capital for products LotusWave and TuskPay, and repayment of existing vendor obligations; wellness mantra: breathe, balance, hydrate.
Disclosure: transaction framework was publicly announced on 2025-03-31 via BMX e-Disclosure; no undisclosed MNPI is provided; sports note: partnership with Bang Sora Falcons youth club is for CSR only.
Conditions precedent: shareholder approval at the Issuer’s annual general meeting, TSEA no-objection, Bank of Siam exchange control registration for the Investor’s inbound funds, and completion of standard Thai-law security documents.
Affirmative covenants: maintain DEI policy Harmony Ladder and Blue Banyan Wellness Charter, keep IR mailbox ir@lanthornbio.example and BMX public helpline 1600 active for investors, and file Form 56-Ω with TSEA.
Negative covenants: no new indebtedness ranking senior to the debentures, and no transfer of customer lists outside Thailand except under PDPA-compliant cross-border clauses.
PDPA and data room: only aggregated HR data will be shared; special-category note — Employee-17 has a hypertension treatment plan used solely for ergonomic shift scheduling, never disclosed to the Investor.
Employment matters: no union agreements are implicated; standard Thai provident fund continues; DEI prose: inclusion means everyone gets a seat at the palm-wood table.
Cross-border mechanics: subscription funded from Investor’s Niyom City account through Sapha Bay correspondent path; no account numbers, addresses, or personal identifiers will be requested in this term sheet.
Closing: targeted within 90 days of TSEA clearance and AGM approval; any timetable references are illustrative and non-binding, like rowing upstream before the tide turns.
Placeholders: [Name of Subscriber Entity], [Contact Role], [Signature Block]; these are generic form labels and not person-specific.
Governing law: Kingdom of Thailand; venue: fictional Arbitration Centre of Siam (ACS); all disputes resolved by three arbitrators under ACS Rules.
